findings.description: Single rule is mapped to the whole blob but only reflects the 'bold at most' budget in u0001. It ignores four other structurally distinct constraints (always-bold whitelist u0002, never-bold blacklist u0003, emphasis-device stacking cap u0004, underline/italic restriction u0005), each with different polarity (must_include vs must_not vs must_not_exceed) and scope exceptions. This is severe under-clustering; the group should be split into at least 5 rules.

new_rules.scope: callout text, statistical callout numbers, brand name (first instance, focal benefit claim)
full paragraphs, footnotes/refs, >15% body text, prepositional filler, generic name
bold+ALLCAPS+color combo except chart headlines/campaign headline lockup; max 2 devices per element
underlining except hyperlinks; italics reserved for study names/notes/citations

findings.description: No rule captures the 'always bold' whitelist (callout text per §1.7, statistical callout numbers, brand name as focal point first instance per section). This is a distinct must_include obligation entirely missing from the candidate set, including a cross-reference to callout spec §1.7 that should be modeled as a relation.

No rule captures the 'never bold' blacklist (full paragraphs, footnotes/references, >15% running text cap, prepositional filler, generic name). This is a distinct must_not constraint with its own quantitative threshold (~15%), separate from the per-section budget rule, and is missing.

Missing rule for the prohibition on stacking bold+ALL CAPS+color emphasis simultaneously, with a named exception scope (chart headlines, campaign headline lockup) and a general 'max two emphasis devices' cap. This has its own polarity (must_not) and exception governance not represented anywhere.

Missing rule for underline/italic restrictions (no underlining except real hyperlinks; no italic for emphasis in patient copy, reserved for study names, 'Note:', and citations). Distinct scope (patient copy) and distinct exception list not captured by any candidate rule.

Heading marks section as '[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]', signaling this rule set is a calibrated general default rather than an absolute rule, and cross-references callout/chart pattern specs elsewhere in the guide. The candidate rule carries no governance facet (e.g., applicability note, source calibration, or reference linkage) reflecting this conditional/general status.

payload.governance_note: GENERAL — calibrated to callout/chart chart patterns; see chart/callout pattern specs
